" Owner: Basis team lead
" ETA: By next maintenance window (2024-03-22)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle a situation where an AMDP returns incorrect data in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Incorrect AMDP results careful investigation require karte hain. Pehle, validate karo ki result actually wrong hai — SE16N count ya known baseline jaise trusted reference se compare karo. Scope identify karo — ek user, ek company code, specific date range. HANA Database Explorer mein, issue reproduce karne ke liye production-like parameters ke saath underlying SQLScript directly run karo. Common causes include NULL handling gaps — calculations ke through unhandled NULLs propagate karte hue — filter conditions too broad ya too narrow, currency calculations mein rounding differences, ya production mein edge case data jo development mein present nahi tha. Development mein fix karo, representative data ke saath thoroughly test karo, aur transport karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Incorrect AMDP results require careful investigation. First, validate that the result is actually wrong — compare with a trusted reference like an SE16N count or known baseline. Identify the scope — one user, one company code, specific date range. In HANA Database Explorer, run the underlying SQLScript directly with production-like parameters to reproduce the issue. Common causes include NULL handling gaps — unhandled NULLs propagating through calculations — filter conditions too broad or too narrow, rounding differences in currency calculations, or edge case data in production not present in development. Fix in development, test thoroughly with representative data, and transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AR aging report mein ek company code ka total wrong tha — 20% high. HANA Explorer mein directly same query run kiya — same result. Phir NULL check kiya — bsid mein kuch rows mein dmbtr NULL tha. ABAP report mein yeh NULL skip hota tha. AMDP mein COALESCE add kiya — rerun kiya — numbers match. Transport kiya fix. Business ne confirmed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: User ke word pe immediately code change karna — pehle verify karo ki result actually wrong hai. Kabhi kabhi user expectation galat hoti hai ya reference data stale hoti hai. Always cross-validate before fixing.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Incorrect data investigation:
" Step 1: Validate issue is real
" Business confirms: expected total = 5.2M, got 4.8M
" Cross-check: SE16N -> bsid -> count open items for bukrs

" Step 2: Reproduce in HANA Explorer
" Run equivalent SQLScript directly in HANA DB Explorer:
" SELECT kunnr, SUM(dmbtr) FROM bsid
"   WHERE bukrs = '1000' AND augbl = ''
"   GROUP BY kunnr;
" Compare with AMDP output

" Step 3: Identify NULL impact
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bsid
  WHERE bukrs = '1000' AND dmbtr IS NULL;
" Found: 1500 rows with NULL dmbtr

" Step 4: Fix - add COALESCE in AMDP SQLScript
" BEFORE:
" SUM(dmbtr) AS total
" AFTER:
" SUM(COALESCE(dmbtr, 0)) AS total

" Step 5: Test with same parameters in DEV
" Transport fix, validate in QA, then PRD

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you build resilience into AMDP methods to prevent future incidents?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP methods mein resilience build karna include karta hai: potentially null columns pe COALESCE use karke NULL-safe calculations. Accidental full-table processing prevent karne ke liye selective WHERE conditions. Result set size awareness — bade expected results ke liye, memory pressure prevent karne ke liye pagination ya limits consider karo. ABAP caller AMDP call ko cx_amdp_error ke liye TRY-CATCH mein meaningful fallback behavior ke saath wrap karta hai. Monitoring ke liye execution time aur record counts logging. NULL input, empty result, aur large data scenarios sahit edge cases cover karne wale ABAP unit tests. Yeh practices AMDP methods ko production edge cases mein more robust banate hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Building resilience into AMDP methods includes: NULL-safe calculations using COALESCE on all potentially null columns. Selective WHERE conditions to prevent accidental full-table processing. Result set size awareness — for large expected results, consider pagination or limits to prevent memory pressure. ABAP caller wrapping AMDP call in TRY-CATCH for cx_amdp_error with meaningful fallback behavior. Logging execution time and record counts for monitoring. ABAP unit tests covering edge cases including NULL input, empty result, and large data scenarios. These practices make AMDP methods more robust in production edge cases.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Resilient AMDP pattern humne follow kiya: ABAP caller mein TRY-CATCH cx_amdp_error tha — agar AMDP fail ho toh fallback message tha. AMDP mein COALESCE sab nullable columns pe. ABAP caller mein execution time log karta tha SLG1 mein. Jab AMDP fail hua HANA maintenance ke baad — fallback message instantly show hua users ko, aur monitoring alert trigger hua. Transparent failure better than silent wrong data.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AMDP ko fire-and-forget karna without exception handling in ABAP caller — agar AMDP fail ho toh ABAP program crash kar jaayega instead of meaningful error. Hamesha cx_amdp_error catch karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Resilient AMDP caller pattern
METHOD get_report_data.
  DATA lo_amdp   TYPE REF TO zcl_ar_aging.
  DATA lt_result TYPE zcl_ar_aging=>tt_aging.
  DATA lv_start  TYPE timestamp.
  GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_start.
  TRY.
    lo_amdp = NEW zcl_ar_aging( ).
    lo_amdp->get_ar_aging(
      IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = iv_bukrs
      EXPORTING et_aging  = lt_result ).
    " Log success
    zcl_app_logger=>log(
      iv_msg    = |AMDP success: { lines( lt_result ) } records|
      iv_obj    = 'AMDP_MONITOR' ).
  CATCH cx_amdp_error INTO DATA(lx_amdp).
    " Log failure
    zcl_app_logger=>log(
      iv_msg    = |AMDP failed: { lx_amdp->get_text( ) }|
      iv_obj    = 'AMDP_MONITOR' ).
    " Graceful degradation - inform user
    MESSAGE 'Report temporarily unavailable. Contact support.'
      TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.
ENDMETHOD.

amdp-hana-031.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: FS matlab Functional Specification — yeh business requirements ka document hai jisme likha hota hai kya banana hai: 'Report mein yeh columns chahiye, yeh filters chahiye, performance 10 seconds mein aani chahiye.' TS matlab Technical Specification — yeh developer ka blueprint hai jisme likha hota hai kaise banana hai: 'AMDP use karenge, ye table se data lenge, SQLScript CASE WHEN se buckets banayenge.' FS to TS conversion ek translation exercise hai — business language ko technical language mein badalna. AMDP context mein yeh especially important hai kyunki business sirf performance requirement deta hai — 'report 1 minute se zyada slow nahi honi chahiye.' Developer decide karta hai ki yeh target AMDP se achieve hoga ya CDS view se ya normal ABAP se. Yeh decision TS mein clearly documented hona chahiye: kyon AMDP choose kiya, kya alternative soche, kya trade-offs hain. FS mein likha hai: 'Finance team ko customer-wise AR aging report chahiye with 30, 60, 90 day buckets.' TS mein developer likhega: 'AMDP ZCL_AR_AGING banayenge, bsid table use karenge, CASE WHEN DAYS_BETWEEN se buckets calculate karenge HANA pe, performance target 20 seconds.'
amdp-hana-031.hinglishMasterExplanation:
FS to TS conversion for AMDP aur HANA optimization ek critical skill hai jo senior ABAP developers se expect kiya jaata hai. Yeh sirf document likhna nahi hai — yeh analysis, design decisions, aur risk assessment hai. Structured approach:

1. FS ko thoroughly read karo:
   - Business requirement kya hai exactly?
   - Performance requirements kya hain — SLA, response time?
   - Data volumes kya hain — table sizes, expected result sets?
   - Frequency — realtime, batch, on-demand?
   - Consumers — Fiori app, ABAP report, background job?
   - Authorization requirements — row-level, field-level?
   - Dependencies — existing systems, interfaces?

2. Technical analysis karo:
   - Source tables identify karo — VBAK, BSID, etc.
   - Table sizes check karo — SE16N counts, production volumes
   - Existing similar reports/programs check karo — reuse opportunity
   - Performance baseline — agar existing solution hai toh benchmark karo

3. Architecture decision — AMDP vs alternatives:
   - Simple aggregation + OData needed -> CDS view preferred
   - Complex conditional multi-step logic -> AMDP
   - Batch job offline processing -> ABAP SELECT with optimized SQL
   - Hybrid: CDS for data model + AMDP for complex calculation
   - Document decision rationale in TS

4. AMDP design decisions:
   - Class name convention — ZCL_<DOMAIN>_AMDP pattern
   - Method name — descriptive, action-based
   - IMPORTING parameters — scalar filters from business requirement
   - EXPORTING parameters — result structure matching report fields
   - USING clause — all tables and CDS views needed
   - OPTIONS READ-ONLY — agar sirf read ho (almost always yes)
   - SQLScript structure — tabular variables plan karo, step by step

5. Authorization design:
   - FS mein authorization requirements:
     'Only users with company code access can see that company code data'
   - TS mein design:
     'AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT Z_BUKRS_ACC in ABAP wrapper before AMDP call'
     'AMDP itself passes bukrs as filter parameter — no cross-company leak'

6. Error handling design:
   - cx_amdp_error handling in ABAP caller
   - Fallback behavior
   - Logging requirements

7. Testing strategy:
   - Unit test cases from FS scenarios
   - Performance test data volume
   - Edge cases: empty result, NULL values, max data

8. Transport planning:
   - Dependency objects list
   - Transport sequence
   - Target system validation steps

9. TS review:
   - Technical reviewer — architect
   - Business validation — does TS address all FS requirements?
   - Sign-off before development starts
amdp-hana-031.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'FS to TS conversion for AMDP starts with thoroughly understanding the business requirement — performance SLA, data volumes, consumer type, and authorization needs. The key design decision is whether AMDP is the right tool: for simple aggregation with OData consumption I prefer CDS views; AMDP is chosen when the logic is complex and procedural — multi-step calculations, conditional transformations, or intermediate tabular variable processing. In the TS I document the AMDP class structure, IMPORTING and EXPORTING parameter types referencing ABAP Dictionary objects, the USING clause dependencies, SQLScript design with step-by-step tabular variable flow, authorization approach at the ABAP wrapper level, error handling strategy, and transport sequence. I also document why AMDP was chosen over alternatives — this helps future maintainers understand the architectural intent.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein FS to TS conversion ka ek concrete example ready rakho. 'FS mein likha tha: AR aging report with 30-60-90 day buckets, under 30 second response, 50 lakh records. TS mein decision: AMDP kyunki CASE WHEN + DAYS_BETWEEN complex conditional logic hai jo CDS mein multi-column aggregation ke saath awkward hoga. Design: ZCL_AR_AGING class, bsid USING, OPTIONS READ-ONLY, ABAP authority-check wrapper.' Yeh specificity dikhata hai genuine experience.
amdp-hana-031.codeExamples:
" FS to TS Conversion Example
" FS Requirement:
" 'AR Aging Report: customer-wise open items in 30/60/90 day
"  buckets. Performance: under 30 seconds. Auth: company code
"  access. Consumer: ABAP ALV report. Data: ~50L bsid rows.'

" TS Design Decision:
" AMDP chosen because:
"  - Multi-column conditional aggregation (4 buckets)
"  - DAYS_BETWEEN per-row calculation before GROUP BY
"  - CDS single SELECT would need 4 separate aggregations
"  - Data volume: pushdown mandatory for <30s target

" TS: AMDP Class Structure
CLASS zcl_ar_aging_amdp DEFINITION PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES: if_amdp_marker_hdb.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_aging_result,
             kunnr    TYPE kna1-kunnr,
             bukrs    TYPE bsid-bukrs,
             bucket_0_30  TYPE dmbtr,
             bucket_31_60 TYPE dmbtr,
             bucket_61_90 TYPE dmbtr,
             bucket_90p   TYPE dmbtr,
           END OF ts_aging_result.
    TYPES: tt_aging_result
      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_aging_result
      WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    METHODS: get_ar_aging
      IMPORTING VALUE(iv_bukrs)   TYPE bukrs
                VALUE(iv_waers)   TYPE waers
      EXPORTING VALUE(et_aging)   TYPE tt_aging_result
      RAISING   cx_amdp_error.
ENDCLASS.
" TS: SQLScript design notes
" Step 1: lt_open = open items from bsid for bukrs
" Step 2: lt_aged = add DAYS_BETWEEN per item
" Step 3: et_aging = GROUP BY kunnr, conditional SUM per bucket
" Auth: ABAP wrapper: AUTHORITY-CHECK Z_FI_BUKRS before call
amdp-hana-031.interviewScriptHinglish: Functional Specification business requirements capture karta hai: outputs, users, frequencies, SLAs, aur compliance constraints. Jab hum AMDP solution design karte hain, FS se data volume, response time SLA, authorization model, aur consumer pattern extract karte hain, phir unhe Technical Specification mein translate karte hain. TS class aur method signatures, IMPORTING aur EXPORTING parameters, SQLScript pseudocode, USING dependencies, authorization wrapper design, error handling, test cases, aur transport sequence cover karta hai. Development start hone se pehle TS pe stakeholder sign-off lete hain. FS aur TS ke beech yeh traceability audit reviews support karti hai aur scope drift avoid karti hai. Business value alignment, governance, aur predictable delivery hai.
amdp-hana-031.interviewScriptEnglish: Functional Specification captures business requirements: outputs, users, frequencies, SLAs, and compliance constraints. When designing an AMDP solution, we extract data volume, response time SLA, authorization model, and consumer pattern from the FS, then translate them into a Technical Specification. The TS covers class and method signatures, IMPORTING and EXPORTING parameters, SQLScript pseudocode, USING dependencies, authorization wrapper design, error handling, test cases, and transport sequence. We obtain stakeholder sign-off on the TS before development begins. This traceability between FS and TS supports audit reviews and avoids scope drift. The business value is alignment, governance, and predictable delivery.

followupAnswerBank.question: What information from an FS do you need to decide if AMDP is required?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS se, AMDP required hai ya nahi decide karne ke liye main dekhta hoon: data volume — kitni rows process honge, kyunki pushdown high volumes ke saath sabse valuable hai. Performance SLA — agar millions of records ke liye 30 seconds ke under required hai, AMDP ke through HANA pushdown likely needed hai. Logic complexity — agar business requirement aging buckets jaisa conditional multi-step calculation, iterative transformations, ya complex date logic involve karta hai, AMDP fit karta hai. Consumer type — agar ABAP report results consume karta hai, AMDP straightforward hai; agar OData needed hai, AMDP wrapping karne wala CDS table function better ho sakta hai. Frequency — realtime interactive reports ko nightly batch jobs se zyada pushdown chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: From an FS, to decide if AMDP is required I look for: data volume — how many rows will be processed, because pushdown is most valuable with high volumes. Performance SLA — if under 30 seconds is required for millions of records, HANA pushdown via AMDP is likely needed. Logic complexity — if the business requirement involves conditional multi-step calculation like aging buckets, iterative transformations, or complex date logic, AMDP fits. Consumer type — if it is an ABAP report consuming results, AMDP is straightforward; if OData is needed, CDS table function wrapping AMDP might be better. Frequency — realtime interactive reports need pushdown more than nightly batch jobs.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein 3 things ne AMDP decision confirm kiya: 50 lakh rows (large volume), 30 second SLA (tight), 4-bucket conditional aggregation (complex). Teen mein se teen checked — AMDP clear choice tha. TS mein CDS view alternative consider kiya, document kiya kyun reject kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FS ko bina pura padhe AMDP assume karna — kabhi kabhi simple report ke liye CDS sufficient hai. Analysis karo pehle, then decide.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS Analysis Checklist for AMDP decision:
" Data Volume: _______rows (from SE16N or DBA)
"   < 100K rows   -> standard ABAP SELECT OK
"   100K - 1M     -> optimized SQL, maybe CDS
"   > 1M rows     -> AMDP pushdown strongly consider

" Performance SLA: _______ seconds
"   > 60 seconds  -> pushdown needed if large volume
"   < 30 seconds  -> pushdown likely required

" Logic Complexity:
"   Simple JOIN + GROUP BY -> CDS view preferred
"   Conditional multi-step -> AMDP preferred
"   Date series generation -> AMDP with SERIES_GENERATE

" Consumer:
"   OData/Fiori -> CDS or CDS table function over AMDP
"   ABAP report -> AMDP direct call
"   Both        -> CDS table function wrapping AMDP

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you translate performance requirements from FS to technical design in TS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS performance requirement ko TS technical design mein translate karna: relevant tables ke liye SE16N ya HANA studio use karke actual data volume measure karo. Agar existing solution exist karta hai, isko baseline ki tarah benchmark karo. Calculate karo ki performance target standard ABAP SQL ke saath achievable hai — agar nahi, AMDP ya CDS ke through pushdown required hai. TS mein document karo: data volume, performance target, kyun pushdown required hai, jo target achieve karta hai woh AMDP design, aur performance test plan. TS mein include karo: QA mein representative data volume ke saath test, HANA execution plan validate kare ki pushdown ho raha hai, actual execution time measure karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Translating FS performance requirement to TS technical design: measure the actual data volume using SE16N or HANA studio for the relevant tables. If an existing solution exists, benchmark it as a baseline. Calculate whether the performance target is achievable with standard ABAP SQL — if not, pushdown via AMDP or CDS is required. In the TS, document: the data volume, the performance target, why pushdown is required, the AMDP design that achieves it, and the performance test plan. Include in TS: test with representative data volume in QA, validate HANA execution plan confirms pushdown, measure actual execution time.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 'Response under 60 seconds.' SE16N mein bsid count: 35 lakh rows. Baseline test: ABAP SELECT LOOP 22 minutes. TS mein: 'Standard ABAP cannot meet 60s SLA with 35L rows. AMDP pushdown required. Expected: GROUP BY on HANA 8-15 seconds. Test plan: QA system 30L row test, measure execution time, validate execution plan.' Architecture justified with numbers.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Performance requirement ko simply copy karna FS se TS mein without technical analysis — TS mein kaise achieve karenge yeh batana zaroori hai, sirf kya requirement hai nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FS to TS: Performance Requirement Translation
" FS Says:
"   'AR aging report must complete in under 60 seconds'

" TS Analysis:
"   Data Volume: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bsid -> 35,000,000 rows
"   Baseline: ABAP SELECT + LOOP -> 22 minutes (NOT meeting SLA)
"   Alternative 1: Optimized ABAP SQL -> ~8 minutes (NOT enough)
"   Alternative 2: CDS view GROUP BY -> ~45 seconds (borderline)
"   Alternative 3: AMDP pushdown -> ~12 seconds (MEETS SLA)

" TS Decision: AMDP ZCL_AR_AGING selected
" Performance Test Plan:
"   - QA system: run with 30L row subset, measure time
"   - Validate: HANA execution plan shows COLUMN STORE access
"   - Success criteria: execution <= 20 seconds with 35L rows
"   - Production: smoke test first run, monitor first week

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you design the IMPORTING and EXPORTING parameters for AMDP in TS?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS mein AMDP parameter design ke liye: IMPORTING parameters FS se user selection filters represent karte hain — company code, date range, fiscal year. Scalar imports ke liye bukrs, gjahr, dats jaise ABAP Dictionary-based types use karo. EXPORTING parameters result structure represent karte hain — FS mein described report ke liye exactly needed fields ke saath Dictionary structure type define karo. Exporting parameter ke liye DEFAULT KEY ke saath standard table type create karo. TS mein document karo: har parameter name, ABAP Dictionary type, purpose, aur kya yeh mandatory hai. AMDP parameters ke liye local ABAP TYPE definitions avoid karo — reliability ke liye Dictionary types use karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For AMDP parameter design in TS: IMPORTING parameters represent user selection filters from the FS — company code, date range, fiscal year. Use ABAP Dictionary-based types like bukrs, gjahr, dats for scalar imports. EXPORTING parameters represent the result structure — define a Dictionary structure type with exactly the fields needed for the report as described in the FS. Create a standard table type with DEFAULT KEY for the exporting parameter. Document in TS: each parameter name, ABAP Dictionary type, purpose, and whether it is mandatory. Avoid local ABAP TYPE definitions for AMDP parameters — use Dictionary types for reliability.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein report columns listed the: kunnr, company code, 4 bucket amounts, currency. TS mein structure design ki: ZS_AR_AGING_RESULT with exactly those fields using Dictionary types — kunnr TYPE kna1-kunnr, bukrs TYPE bsid-bukrs, amounts TYPE dmbtr. Table type ZTT_AR_AGING_RESULT TYPE STANDARD TABLE of ZS_AR_AGING_RESULT. Activation aur ABAP integration smooth raha.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Local TYPE definition use karna for AMDP EXPORTING parameter — can cause activation issues or type incompatibility. Always create Dictionary structure and table type in SE11.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS: Parameter Design Documentation
" IMPORTING Parameters:
"   iv_bukrs  TYPE bukrs     - Company code (mandatory)
"                              From: Selection screen BUKRS field
"   iv_gjahr  TYPE gjahr     - Fiscal year (mandatory)
"                              From: Selection screen GJAHR field
"   iv_waers  TYPE waers     - Currency (optional, default 'USD')
"                              From: Selection screen optional field

" EXPORTING Parameters:
"   et_aging  TYPE ztt_ar_aging_result
"   Structure: ZS_AR_AGING_RESULT (create in SE11)
"     kunnr       TYPE kna1-kunnr   - Customer number
"     bukrs       TYPE bsid-bukrs   - Company code
"     bucket_0_30 TYPE dmbtr        - 0-30 day bucket amount
"     bucket_30_60 TYPE dmbtr       - 31-60 day bucket amount
"     bucket_60_90 TYPE dmbtr       - 61-90 day bucket amount
"     bucket_90p  TYPE dmbtr        - 90+ day bucket amount
"   Table type: ZTT_AR_AGING_RESULT
"     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZS_AR_AGING_RESULT
"     WITH DEFAULT KEY  (create in SE11)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document SQLScript design in the Technical Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS mein SQLScript design ek step-by-step logic blueprint ke roop mein document hota hai, full code nahi. Har tabular variable aur uska purpose document karo: yeh kya data hold karta hai aur kaunsa step represent karta hai. FS requirements ko reference karte hue har step pe filter conditions document karo. Aggregate logic document karo — GROUP BY fields, per bucket SUM conditions. Used built-in functions document karo — DAYS_BETWEEN, COALESCE — aur kyun. Yeh pseudo-design technical peers aur architects dono ke through reviewable hai actual SQLScript code read karne ki need ke bina. Yeh implementation guide ki tarah bhi serve karta hai ensure karte hue ki developer design ke saath aligned rahe.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SQLScript design in TS is documented as step-by-step logic blueprint, not full code. Document each tabular variable and its purpose: what data it holds and which step it represents. Document filter conditions at each step referencing FS requirements. Document aggregate logic — GROUP BY fields, SUM conditions per bucket. Document built-in functions used — DAYS_BETWEEN, COALESCE — and why. This pseudo-design is reviewable by both technical peers and architects without needing to read actual SQLScript code. It also serves as implementation guide ensuring the developer stays aligned with the design.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS mein SQLScript design table format mein likhi thi — 4 rows, har row mein step number, tabular variable name, purpose, data source, filter conditions. Architect ne review kiya bina SQLScript knowledge ke bhi — logic flow clear tha. Developer implementation mein same structure follow kiya — minimal deviation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS mein full SQLScript copy karna — TS early stage document hai jab code nahi likha gaya. Logic intent document karo, code development phase mein aayega.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS: SQLScript Design Documentation (Pseudo-design)

" Step 1: lt_open_items
"   Source: bsid
"   Filter: bukrs = :iv_bukrs AND augbl = '' (open items)
"   Fields: belnr, kunnr, dmbtr, bldat
"   Purpose: Base dataset of open AR items

" Step 2: lt_aged
"   Source: :lt_open_items
"   Add: DAYS_BETWEEN(bldat, CURRENT_DATE) AS age_days
"   Fields: all from lt_open_items + age_days
"   Purpose: Calculate age per item

" Step 3: et_aging (final output)
"   Source: :lt_aged
"   GROUP BY: kunnr, bukrs
"   Aggregations:
"     bucket_0_30:  SUM(CASE WHEN age_days <= 30 THEN dmbtr ELSE 0)
"     bucket_31_60: SUM(CASE WHEN age_days BETWEEN 31 AND 60 ...)
"     bucket_61_90: SUM(CASE WHEN age_days BETWEEN 61 AND 90 ...)
"     bucket_90p:   SUM(CASE WHEN age_days > 90 THEN dmbtr ELSE 0)
"   NULL handling: COALESCE on dmbtr before CASE WHEN
"   Purpose: Final bucketed aging summary per customer

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document authorization design in TS for an AMDP-based solution?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP-based solution ke liye TS mein authorization design: document karo ki ABAP AUTHORITY-CHECK AMDP SQLScript body ke andar place nahi ho sakti. Authorization enforcement design ABAP wrapper level pe hai — AMDP call karne wala method pehle specific field values ke saath relevant authorization object check karta hai. AMDP khud authorized value ko IMPORTING parameter filter ke roop mein receive karta hai — example, sirf authorized bukrs pass hoti hai, cross-company data access prevent karte hue. Document karo: kaunsa authorization object check hota hai, kaunsa ABAP method check perform karta hai, authorization failure pe kya hota hai, aur kaise AMDP parameters data isolation ensure karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization design in TS for AMDP: document that ABAP AUTHORITY-CHECK cannot be placed inside AMDP SQLScript body. The authorization enforcement design is at the ABAP wrapper level — the method that calls AMDP first checks the relevant authorization object with the specific field values. The AMDP itself receives the authorized value as an IMPORTING parameter filter — for example, only the authorized bukrs is passed, preventing cross-company data access. Document: which authorization object is checked, which ABAP method performs the check, what happens on authorization failure, and how AMDP parameters ensure data isolation.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein requirement: 'Users can only see their assigned company code data.' TS mein design: ABAP wrapper ZCL_AR_AGING_CALLER=>GET_REPORT performs AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK' with BUKRS = iv_bukrs, ACTVT = '03'. On success, calls AMDP with same iv_bukrs. AMDP SQLScript: WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs — no other company code possible. Architect approved — clean separation of concerns.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS mein authorization section blank chhod dena — security design gap. Har AMDP-based solution mein authorization design explicitly documented hona chahiye — AMDP limitation samajh ke ABAP layer pe design karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS: Authorization Design Section

" FS Requirement:
"   'Users may only view AR data for company codes
"    assigned to them in role-based authorization.'

" TS Authorization Design:
" Location: ABAP wrapper method, NOT inside AMDP
" Object: F_BKPF_BUK (company code authorization)
" Fields: BUKRS (company code), ACTVT ('03' = display)

" ABAP Wrapper Code Design:
METHOD get_ar_report.                 " ABAP wrapper
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    FIELD 'BUKRS' ID iv_bukrs
    FIELD 'ACTVT' ID '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_ar_no_auth
      EXPORTING iv_bukrs = iv_bukrs.
  ENDIF.
  " Only on authorization success:
  lo_amdp = NEW zcl_ar_aging_amdp( ).
  lo_amdp->get_ar_aging(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = iv_bukrs     " authorized value passed
    EXPORTING et_aging  = et_aging ).
  " AMDP WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs ensures no cross-company leak
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What test cases do you define in TS for an AMDP method?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP method ke liye TS mein defined test cases include karte hain: Happy path — company code ke liye known open items, manually calculated expected values ke against bucket amounts verify karo. Empty result — no open items wala company code, empty result set verify karo error nahi. NULL handling — NULL dmbtr test row introduce karo, verify karo ki yeh zero ki tarah treated ho NULL ki tarah propagate na ho. Boundary values — exactly 30 days old item bucket 0-30 mein hona chahiye 31-60 mein nahi. Performance test — QA mein full data volume ke saath run karo, SLA ke against execution time measure karo. Authorization test — unauthorized company code ko zcx_ar_no_auth raise karna chahiye. Yeh test cases development ke dauran ABAP unit tests ban jaate hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Test cases defined in TS for an AMDP method include: Happy path — known open items for a company code, verify bucket amounts against manually calculated expected values. Empty result — company code with no open items, verify empty result set not an error. NULL handling — introduce a NULL dmbtr test row, verify it is treated as zero not propagated as NULL. Boundary values — item exactly 30 days old should be in bucket 0-30 not 31-60. Performance test — run with full data volume in QA, measure execution time against SLA. Authorization test — unauthorized company code should raise zcx_ar_no_auth. These test cases become ABAP unit tests during development.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS mein 8 test cases define kiye the. Developer ne ABAP unit tests mein exactly those test cases implement kiye — test-driven approach. Jab NULL handling issue production mein aaya — humara unit test mein NULL test case tha aur woh development mein pass ho raha tha. Difference tha production mein actual NULL data pattern. Post-incident test case update kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf happy path test case TS mein rakhna — edge cases aur NULL handling test mandatory hain. Production incidents often happen in edge cases not covered by tests.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS: Test Case Definitions

" TC01: Happy Path
"   Input: iv_bukrs = '1000', iv_gjahr = '2024'
"   Expected: et_aging has rows with correct bucket amounts
"   Method: Cross-check with SE16N manual calculation

" TC02: Empty Result
"   Input: iv_bukrs = '9999' (no open items)

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you plan the transport sequence in TS for AMDP-dependent objects?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS mein transport sequence section sab create kiye jaane wale objects, unke types, dependencies, aur recommended import order list karta hai. Yeh dependency graph ko bottom-up order mein document karta hai: pehle SE11 structures, phir CDS views ya dependent Dictionary objects, phir last mein AMDP class. Har object ke liye: object name, type, yeh kin objects pe depend karta hai, aur AMDP class import hone se pehle yeh target systems mein hona chahiye. Yeh section QA aur production transports ke liye import runbook ke roop mein Basis team ke saath share hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The transport sequence section in TS lists all objects to be created, their types, dependencies, and recommended import order. It documents the dependency graph in bottom-up order: SE11 structures first, then CDS views or dependent Dictionary objects, then the AMDP class last. For each object: object name, type, which objects it depends on, and the target systems it must be in before the AMDP class is imported. This section is shared with the Basis team as the import runbook for QA and production transports.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS mein transport plan table thi: Row 1 — ZS_AR_AGING_RESULT (structure), no dependency, first import. Row 2 — ZTT_AR_AGING_RESULT (table type), depends on Row 1, second import. Row 3 — ZI_AR_BASE_VIEW (CDS view), depends on Row 1, third import. Row 4 — ZCL_AR_AGING_AMDP (AMDP class), depends on all above, last import. Basis team got exactly this table as their import runbook.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Transport section ko TS mein include nahi karna — developers develop kar lete hain aur transport sequence document karna bhool jaate hain. Last minute mein manual coordination time waste karta hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you get sign-off on a TS for an AMDP-based solution?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP-based solution ke liye TS sign-off involve karta hai: FS checklist ke against developer self-review — har FS requirement TS design element pe mapped. Senior developer ya architect ke through technical peer review — AMDP design decisions, SQLScript approach, authorization design, performance approach, aur transport plan validate karta hai. Business analyst confirmation ki TS output fields aur logic FS requirements ke saath match karte hain. Security review agar FS mein regulatory ya sensitive data requirements hain. Document approvals — email thread ya Confluence comment sufficient hai. Sirf TS sign off hone ke baad development start karo — coding start hone ke baad changes expensive hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TS sign-off for an AMDP-based solution involves: developer self-review against FS checklist — every FS requirement mapped to TS design element. Technical peer review by a senior developer or architect — validate AMDP design decisions, SQLScript approach, authorization design, performance approach, and transport plan. Business analyst confirmation that the TS output fields and logic match the FS requirements. Security review if the FS has regulatory or sensitive data requirements. Document approvals — email thread or Confluence comment is sufficient. Only begin development after TS is signed off — changes after coding has started are expensive.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS review mein architect ne ek important catch kiya: AMDP USING clause mein ZI_BSID_OPEN CDS view listed tha lekin TS mein CDS view ka design nahi tha — assumption tha ki pehle se exist karta hai. Architect ne bola — create karo ya existing use karo clearly document karo. TS update kiya, existing ZI_BSID_BASE use kiya. Sign-off ke baad coding shuru ki — smooth development.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS sign-off skip karna to save time — design issues coding mein fix karna 10x expensive hai. TS review investment hai, tax nahi.
